The Weight
of Place

地 之 重
Land Speaks. 当土地开口说话。
Manifesto
Five films. Five landscapes. One question.
A river in Hunan that cradles a girl.
A mansion in Shanxi that swallows a woman.
A moor in Yorkshire that tears two souls apart.
A loess plateau where an old man keeps a song alive.
A sacred mountain where a family walks two thousand miles on their knees.
The Weight of Place is a film festival about what happens when fragile lives meet enduring lands — when a person, small and brief, collides with a place that was here long before them, and will remain long after.
Place is not a backdrop.
Place is what reveals us.
Festival Trailer
WATCH ON YOUTUBE

Curatorial Statement

We inhabit a condition of suspension—uncertain of where we belong. The Weight of Place asks what the land does to us. How does a piece of earth shape the fate of a human life? In these five films, landscape arrives before the human figure. The land is not a setting; it is the author.

Tracing relationships of immersion, entrapment, and return, we invite you to look again at the places that shape and finally call us back.

The Programme

01 / Prologue

Border Town

1984 | Director: Ling Zifeng
On the banks of a river in remote West Hunan, a ferryman's granddaughter named Cuicui grows up entwined with the water and mountains around her. Adapted from Shen Congwen's novella, the film moves at the rhythm of the water itself. Quiet and utterly of her landscape, she ferries strangers across the river while a slow, fragile love unfolds.

In This Programme

It captures a vanishing state: the human being not yet separated from the land. The land arrives first. It always does.

02 / Part I

Raise the Red Lantern

1991 | Director: Zhang Yimou
Inside a high-walled Shanxi household, power is distributed through a nightly ritual of lighting red lanterns. Songlian, a university student turned fourth wife, is gradually drawn into its cruel logic as the architecture turns into a closed structure of power that slowly consumes her.

In This Programme

The courtyard does not protect but consumes. Watch how the architecture itself does the violence: the symmetry, the high walls, the shrinking sky.

Killed by Walls
Catherine is undone by architecture. She enters a world of wealth and refinement, only to find the courtyard of civilization consumes her.
THE WATERSHED
Claimed by Wind
Heathcliff is claimed by the moor. From the same landscape arises a destiny of wildness—two opposing souls from one single land.
03 / The Watershed

Wuthering Heights

2026 | Director: Emerald Fennell
Following the intense bond between Heathcliff and Catherine on the Yorkshire moors. Catherine chooses social respectability, while Heathcliff is claimed by the wind. The moor becomes the force that binds, wounds, and refuses to let them go.

In This Programme

This is the centre where the programme turns. From a single landscape, two opposing fates: Catherine is killed by walls and Heathcliff is claimed by wind.

04 / Part II

Song of the Phoenix

2016 | Director: Wu Tianming
On the Loess Plateau, an aging suona master searches for a worthy apprentice. As village life changes rapidly and young people leave for cities, this final film becomes a quiet elegy for an instrument, a teacher, and a disappearing way of belonging to the land.

In This Programme

This film marks the movement toward return. In the master's vision, his body gradually merges with the yellow earth of the plateau.

05 / Coda

Paths of the Soul

2015 | Director: Zhang Yang
A group of villagers begin a long pilgrimage to Lhasa and Mount Kailash. They travel through full body prostration across thousands of kilometres. What emerges is a portrait of faith and endurance, where the human body and the road become inseparable.

In This Programme

The journey ends where the question dissolves. The land does not receive them; it recognises them. The human is the land itself.

Curator's Note
策 展 人 语

Discover why we believe the land speaks before the human story begins.

What place has shaped you?
哪 一 个 地 方 塑 造 了 你 ？
Every place leaves a weight, a memory, a direction.

A CURATED PROGRAMME BY THE CURATORIAL TEAM
×

Curator's Note

We curated this festival because we kept seeing the same image across different films: before a person enters the frame, the land is already there.

A river, a courtyard, a moor, a plateau, a mountain. These places are never just backgrounds. They shape how people love, suffer, wait, resist, and return.

The Weight of Place asks a simple question: how does a place write a human life? We do not think you need to be a film scholar to feel this. You only need to remember a place that has shaped you.

—— The Curatorial Team
